concerns: CSS import strategy is ambiguous — plan says 'add to global.css or import directly in module file' but should commit to one approach. Recommend direct import in threat-model-module.ts to match permission-debt pattern and keep global.css lean.

checkPermissions() can reject — the plan's step 3 must wrap all async data collection in try/catch with sensible fallbacks (empty arrays for permissions/inferences), matching the defensive pattern used in shadow-profile-module.ts.

Shoulder-surfing rule depends on window.screen dimensions — tests will need Object.defineProperty overrides in beforeEach to set screen.width/height in jsdom, which is doable but should be called out in test setup.

Plan step 5 says 'after shadow profile rendering' but should specify exact insertion point — in main.ts the modules are appended sequentially, so the import and append call must be placed after the shadowProfile block to maintain correct dependency order.
